name: Coast Guard TRACEN Yorktown
tagline: A major Coast Guard training center and officer candidate school on Virginia's historic peninsula.
_note: Coast Guard Training Center Yorktown (York County, VA Peninsula, near Williamsburg/Newport News). CONUS full template (BAH MHA VA297 Hampton/Newport News; RentCast/SchoolDigger work). CG uses same BAH tables. Major training center: Officer Candidate School (OCS), 'C' schools, maritime law enforcement. Staff + students. Medical: CG clinic + TRICARE network + nearby DoD MTFs. Facts verified from official sources (uscg.mil, TRICARE, CGX, Wikipedia for corroborated headline facts). Virginia is TRICARE East. Medium-confidence flags: base coords (approx), off-base ZIP 23692 (Yorktown).

data.Trains: Officer Candidate School (OCS), advanced 'C' schools, and maritime law enforcement
data.Nearby: Williamsburg, Newport News, and the Hampton Roads area

sections.title: Mission & Identity
data: Training Center Yorktown is a major Coast Guard schoolhouse, home to Officer Candidate School and a range of advanced ('C') schools and maritime law enforcement training. It runs on a steady flow of students and the staff who train them.
It's on Virginia's historic peninsula near Yorktown and Williamsburg, at the edge of the Hampton Roads region, with an affordable-for-the-coast cost of living and lots of history.

sections.title: Historic Peninsula Living, Affordable and Family-Friendly

data.subtitle: The Virginia Peninsula is affordable and very military-friendly. The main choice is which town fits your commute and schools, with York County well regarded.
intro: The area is reasonably priced and used to military families. Coast Guard housing is limited, so most families live off base across the peninsula.
Where you live mostly comes down to commute and schools across Yorktown, Williamsburg, and Newport News, with the wider Hampton Roads area nearby.

recommendations.advice: York County (Yorktown, Tabb, Grafton) sits right by the base with well-regarded schools.
Williamsburg offers history and family-friendly neighborhoods a short drive away.
Newport News and the wider peninsula add housing and amenities.
Some military housing exists; check early, but plan to live off base.

sections.title: Gates & Access
data: Visitors without a DoD/CG ID need to check in at the visitor control point.
Gate hours and visitor requirements change, so confirm the current details on the official Coast Guard site before you go.

sections.title: On-Base Housing
data: TRACEN Yorktown has some military family housing for staff; Coast Guard housing is limited, so many families live off base across the peninsula.
Eligibility and availability vary, so start with the Coast Guard housing office for current options and waitlists.

source.label: DoD 2026 BAH Rates, MHA VA297 (Hampton/Newport News) — Coast Guard uses the same BAH tables

sections.title: Schools
data: There is no DoDEA district here; children attend the local civilian public schools. York County School Division (well regarded), Williamsburg-James City County, and Newport News serve military families depending on where you live.
Your assigned schools depend on your address, so confirm the district and campus for any neighborhood you are considering.

data.Care: On-base clinic care for the assigned population; family care via the TRICARE network and nearby DoD facilities in Hampton Roads

data.Commissary: Access to Hampton Roads DoD commissaries (Langley-Eustis and others)

intro: Whether to rent or buy is the biggest money decision of most PCS moves, and the affordable, military-friendly peninsula makes buying a real option, especially with repeat tours.
rent: Your tour is short or you're here for a training pipeline.
You want time to learn the peninsula before committing.
You'd rather not gamble on resale timing when you PCS out.
Your BAH comfortably covers rent in the areas you'd consider.
buy: You expect a longer staff tour or repeat Hampton Roads assignments.
You want to use your VA loan and build equity instead of paying rent.
The affordable peninsula market fits your budget for a home you like.
You've found a town and commute you're confident about.
data.bottom_line: There is no universal right answer, but the affordable, military-heavy peninsula makes buying worth a look for longer tours. Run your BAH against the rent and home prices above, weigh your tour length, and talk to a lender and an agent who work with military families before you commit.

intro: The first month at a new duty station is mostly logistics. Knocking these out early makes everything else easier.

steps.title: Line up temporary lodging
steps.text: Book lodging or a short-term rental before you arrive. You can claim Temporary Lodging Expense (TLE) for a limited number of days while you house-hunt.

steps.title: In-process and update DEERS
steps.text: Report to your unit and finance, and make sure DEERS and ID cards are current for every family member so TRICARE and base access work.

steps.title: Check housing
steps.text: Get the housing office's read on York County, Williamsburg, and Newport News before you sign anything.

steps.title: Register your vehicle and sort access
steps.text: Handle base access and Virginia vehicle registration, and learn the visitor process for guests. Bring your registration and insurance.

steps.title: Enroll the kids in school
steps.text: Contact the gaining district (York County, WJCC, or Newport News) early and bring records and immunizations.

steps.title: Set up healthcare
steps.text: Update your TRICARE enrollment and pick a network provider; nearby Hampton Roads DoD facilities are available for some care.

data.a: It's a major Coast Guard training center, home to Officer Candidate School and many advanced ('C') schools plus maritime law enforcement training.

data.q: Do Coast Guard families get BAH?
data.a: Yes, the same BAH tables as the rest of the military. Yorktown is MHA VA297 (Hampton/Newport News).
Mostly off base across the peninsula. York County is closest with strong schools; Williamsburg and Newport News are popular too.
Yes, for a coastal area, and it's very military-friendly, so many families buy.
